title: OpenAI board removes and reinstates Sam Altman
description: OpenAI's non-profit board removed CEO Sam Altman without prior notice to major investors including Microsoft. Employees threatened mass resignation, and Altman was reinstated five days later with a restructured board.

title: DeepSeek releases the R1 reasoning model
description: Chinese AI lab DeepSeek released the R1 reasoning model, reporting a training cost well below comparable US frontier models and built on export-restricted hardware. US technology equities, including NVIDIA, saw a sharp single-day decline following the release.

title: XcodeGhost malware found in App Store apps
description: A modified version of Apple's Xcode developer tool, distributed through unofficial mirrors in China, was found to inject malware into any app compiled with it. Over 40 apps distributed through the App Store, including widely used consumer apps, were affected before Apple removed them.

title: Apple launches Vision Pro
description: Apple launched its Vision Pro spatial computing headset at a $3,500 price point. Several major streaming and media companies declined to build native apps for the platform at launch.

title: Epic Games sues Apple over App Store payment rules
description: Epic Games added a direct payment option to Fortnite in violation of App Store rules, triggering an immediate removal from the App Store. Epic had prepared litigation and a public campaign in advance, and the resulting antitrust case continued in US courts for years afterward.
